Title: Annotate Traffic Images with Vehicle Bounding Boxes
Brief Description: Draw bounding boxes around all vehicles (cars, trucks, motorcycles, buses) in traffic surveillance images to train autonomous driving systems.
Background: We're developing computer vision systems for autonomous vehicles and traffic monitoring. Your annotations will help train AI models to accurately detect and classify vehicles in real-world traffic scenarios.
Objectives: Draw precise bounding boxes around all vehicles in 100 traffic images
Classify each vehicle by type (car, truck, motorcycle, bus)
Ensure complete coverage of all visible vehicles

Step-by-Step Instructions.instruction: Examine each image carefully to identify all vehicles

For each vehicle, draw a bounding box that: 
 1. Completely encloses the vehicle 
 2. Follows the vehicle's orientation 
 3. Includes the entire vehicle (no parts cut off) 
 4. Is as tight as possible around the vehicle
Classify each vehicle using the provided categories: CAR, TRUCK, MOTORCYCLE, BUS
Handle overlapping vehicles by drawing separate boxes
Include partially visible vehicles if more than 50% is visible

Technical Requirements.Tool: Use the provided annotation tool (LabelImg, CVAT, or similar)
Technical Requirements.Format: Save annotations in YOLO format (.txt files).
Other: Complete all 100 images in the dataset

Quality Standards: All vehicles properly bounded with accurate boxes
Correct vehicle classification for each box
Consistent box quality across all images
Proper handling of overlapping and occluded vehicles
Review Checklist: All visible vehicles have bounding boxes
Boxes completely enclose vehicles without gaps
Box quality is consistent across all images
